hero_subtitle: SFTY connects adverse media results with sanctions screening and AML case workflows enabling VASPs to manage risk, maintain audit evidence, and streamline adverse media reviews.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Adverse media reviews slow compliance workflows
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to integrate adverse media results into AML case workflows, causing delays and duplicated efforts. This fragmentation reduces risk visibility and weakens traceability between alerts, reviews, and audit evidence.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates adverse media provider outputs with AML alerts, case reviews, decision logs and evidence, creating one operational workflow that accelerates adverse media risk assessment and strengthens audit traceability.
workflow_title: From adverse media alerts to case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY collects adverse media alerts from multiple providers and guides compliance teams through risk review, case creation, decision logging and evidence preservation.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond adverse media alerts. One compliance layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY turns isolated adverse media checks into structured reviews, cases, decisions and evidence linked to broader AML workflows for clearer compliance control.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs managing adverse media within AML operations by connecting provider outputs, preserving audit evidence, enabling bank due diligence and supporting enterprise deployment control.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support adverse media screening workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how adverse media alerts create case review workflows
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit evidence supports adverse media compliance reviews

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for VASP adverse media screening?
faq_1_answer: SFTY centralizes adverse media checks within AML workflows, structuring alerts, case reviews, decision logs and audit evidence to support VASP compliance operations efficiently.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle adverse media alerts?
faq_2_answer: SFTY routes adverse media alerts into structured cases, linking them with customer profiles, risk rules and review histories to streamline triage and escalation within AML operations.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve adverse media evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves adverse media case history, decision records and supporting evidence to support a clear, auditable trail for compliance reviews and regulator readiness.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance tools?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY connects with existing KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening tools to connect provider outputs into one operational compliance workflow without disrupting current setups.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace adverse media screening providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY works above existing screening providers, transforming their outputs into structured workflows and audit evidence rather than replacing underlying adverse media services.
final_title: Connect adverse media results into case workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY structures adverse media checks into AML cases, evidence preservation and regulatory rule updates for VASPs.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
